reasoning: The question asks whether there will be >10 times the baseline 'Violence against civilians' in Republic of Congo (Brazzaville) during the 30 days before 2026-03-08. With a baseline historically at 0 events per month (reference value 1.000), this requires at least 11 discrete 'Violence against civilians' events to occur between early February and March 7, 2026. ACLED highly strictly defines this category as attacks on non-combatants distinct from protests/riots. A review of news up to March 1, 2026, shows that President Sassou Nguesso's campaign kicked off peacefully on February 28. No major outbreaks of armed violence, insurgency, or state terror targeting civilians outside protests have been reported recently. To hit 11 standalone events in a matter of days before March 7, given the complete lack of such events over the prior year, is practically impossible absent an undetected, massively unreported breakout of armed conflict. Therefore, the probability is at the minimum threshold (0.02).
